title: ranunculus lacer
intro: Ranunculus lacer is a flowering plant species commonly known as the lacerated buttercup, which belongs to the ranunculaceae family. The plant contains alkaloids, such as protoanemonin, which possess medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. Ranunculus lacer has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including skin conditions, wounds, and gastrointestinal problems, due to its antibacterial and antifungal properties. The plant can be prepared in herbal teas, infusions, or salves for topical application, and its dried flowers can be used in homeopathy.
